summary: Validated the deploy fix that adds frontend/yarn.lock (and .env.example, rsync-free safe-pull.sh) to the git index so Bluehost Docker builds can locate them. All 9 git/filesystem checks pass.

checks_executed.actual: 6fe7561 fix(deploy): track frontend/yarn.lock so Docker build can copy it

checks_executed.check: git diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- frontend/yarn.lock | head -3

checks_executed.actual: # THIS IS AN AUTOGENERATED FILE... + # yarn lockfile v1 (Classic yarn)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 was a pure git-index/filesystem validation of the yarn.lock tracking fix (commit 6fe7561). yarn.lock is Classic yarn v1 format, 572 KB, now tracked. .env.example is tracked. safe-pull.sh is rsync-free. The next user-facing step is to Save-to-Github → re-run safe-pull on Bluehost → re-run ./deploy.sh; Docker build should now succeed at step 3/6.
rca of the issue: frontend/yarn.lock was present on the preview disk but never `git add`-ed, so GitHub didn't have it and Bluehost's git pull couldn't deliver it. Dockerfile step 3/6 `COPY package.json yarn.lock ./` therefore failed with `/yarn.lock: not found`. Fix: committed yarn.lock (and .env.example, rsync-free safe-pull.sh) in commit 6fe7561. Verified all four artifacts are now tracked and well-formed.
